hypothesis: If a Transformer encoder-decoder with 150 cm⁻¹ patch embeddings is pretrained on simulated IR spectra and fine-tuned on NIST while jointly optimizing a spectral reconstruction alignment loss across mixture ratios, then it will accurately identify the constituent molecules (SMILES) of binary and ternary IR mixture spectra—including experimentally measured ATR-IR mixtures—with higher Top-k accuracy and Tanimoto similarity than models trained without the reconstruction alignment because the joint objective disentangles pure-component features from mixed spectra.
